title: <div>  •  Whiplash (Film) – Artistic obsession, mentorship, and the cost of grea
content:
• Whiplash (Film) – Artistic obsession, mentorship, and the cost of greatness.
• Real (Manga by Takehiko Inoue) – Basketball, struggle, and finding identity through hardship.
• Vagabond (Manga by Takehiko Inoue) – Samurai epic, personal philosophy, and defining true strength.
• Fourth Inspiration: The Climber (Shin-ichi Sakamoto) – A psychological sports drama about isolation and self-discovery through climbing.

👉 Why These Matter:
These works share high-stakes personal journeys, intense psychological exploration, and an emphasis on self-improvement at all costs.

2.2 Beat-by-Beat Analysis of Your Inspirations

Let’s break each one down using story structure principles so you can see the mechanics behind the emotion.

Whiplash – Three-Act Breakdown

Beat Description
Opening Image Andrew, alone in a practice room, striving for perfection.
Setup He wants to be great but lacks confidence.
Inciting Incident Fletcher recruits him, but immediately humiliates him.
context: From document: The Graphic Novel Writing Guide for Derek: Inspiration, Structure… — chunk 19 (chunk 19/22)
